opp.ais_why:
• The March 31 2026 demo confirmed alignment on the AI value proposition: consolidated spend analytics, AI-driven CLM and autonomous negotiation were validated against Cinemark's pain points. The buyer acknowledged the AI fit but IT raised a governance constraint requiring AI models to run in a private tenant with no Cinemark data leaving that environment. That is sophisticated AI engagement with guardrails.
• Buyer questions on AutoClass AI classification, Merlin intake orchestration via Teams, MS Word InstaReview for clause-level risk, and Anna autonomous negotiation for tail-spend were active but reactive to the vendor flow. SVP Jon Gappa was in the room with Senior Directors Damasio and Flowers, with the close framed as comparing Zycus against other solutions rather than committing to AI as the differentiator.
• AI importance is rated Neutral and the description records no AI adoption currently with prospect curious in an ROI-driven posture. Four meetings on record, including a deep-dive 89-minute session, but the messaging is about building a generic procurement business case rather than an AI-led transformation. With no AI champion, this sits cleanly on the Curious side.

champion.narrative: Leandro Damasio (Sr Director Strategic Sourcing, Contact Role) is the confirmed, active champion. He initiated contact via LinkedIn (discovery call notes 3 Mar and 6 Mar reference repeated scheduling coordination through LinkedIn due to email deliverability issues), led both discovery calls (6 Mar and 31 Mar demo), articulated detailed requirements unprompted, brought 6 additional stakeholders (including his SVP and peer Senior Director) to the 90-minute demo on 31 Mar, and has maintained continuous engagement through June (Next Step notes rep follow-ups 15 Apr, 22 Apr, 4 May, 12 May, 20 May, 10 Jun, all with Leandro). He has direct access to the Economic Buyer (Jon Gappa, SVP, his direct or skip-level manager) and brought him to the demo. Evidence of advocacy: Leandro proactively shared detailed pain points and requirements on both discovery calls without prompting, accepted Karson's offer to help with RFP templates and business case support on 31 Mar, and continues to respond to rep outreach (4 May per Next Step: 'leandro got back to me mentioning they were busy, looking at my response this week'). Current engagement: active as of 10 Jun, though he was out-of-office until mid-Jun per Next Step ('FU with leandro he is ooo until friday' dated 10 Jun, implying return ~14 Jun; LastActivityDate 18 Jun confirms resumed activity). No signs of cooling. Leandro is strong, engaged, and driving the process, but he is NOT the final Economic Buyer (Duane stated business case must be approved by 'the organization,' signaling Leandro does not control budget). Champion strength is high; the gap is converting Leandro's operational sponsorship into executive-level (Jon Gappa or above) commercial and timeline commitment.

competition.narrative: Cinemark is running a multi-vendor evaluation leading to a formal RFP, but no specific competitor names have been disclosed by the buyer on any call or in any Salesforce field. Leandro stated on 31 Mar 'we are evaluating a few other tools as well, just in the beginning trying to understand what's out there in the market' and confirmed 'I will probably go to a formal RFP,' establishing that Zycus is one of multiple vendors being benchmarked. Duane added on the same call that 'the majority of the sourcing team are recent hires seeking to benchmark Zycus capabilities,' reinforcing that this is a comparative evaluation, not a sole-source selection. The scope (Spend Analytics + CLM + autonomous tail-spend negotiation, integrated with Workday, replacing manual Excel and Smartsheet processes) narrows the field to enterprise source-to-contract platforms; likely competitors include Coupa (spend management and CLM), Ivalua (source-to-pay suite), SAP Ariba (if they can show non-ERP modules), Jaggaer (CLM and sourcing), and GEP (spend analytics and sourcing). The buyer has not named any, so competitive intelligence is inference only at this stage. Zycus's wedge: organic platform on a single data core enabling tight spend-to-contract enforcement (vs. stitched acquisitions), AI agents (autonomous negotiation, InstaReview clause risk) as differentiation, and Microsoft Word + Teams + Workday integration matching Cinemark's stated environment. Demo feedback was positive ('definitely covers the vast majority of our pain points' per 31 Mar notes), and Zycus has met every stated criterion, but the buyer is still in discovery across the vendor shortlist and has not indicated a leader. The RFP will be the competitive gate. Winning move: co-author the business case with quantified ROI (savings from vendor rationalization, cycle time reduction, contract compliance lift, tail-spend capture) to make Zycus the vendor that helps Cinemark secure budget, not just a compliant RFP respondent.

identify_pain.narrative: Leandro (Sr Director Strategic Sourcing, the project lead and operational pain owner) articulated specific, high-impact operational and financial pain across both discovery calls. On 6 Mar he described fragmented spend data: 'we have Workday as our ERP, plus satellite softwares for POs and inventory at theater level for food and beverage, plus Service Channel for MROs, and all that feeds Workday financials. The information that gets to Workday is basically invoice amount, not the details, which makes my life really hard when I have to do spend analysis and consolidate all that data.' He named the manual effort required: 'if you ask me what's your contract compliance rate today, I don't know. I had to really deep dive on the data spend, probably one or two days getting Excel files and cross information to get there.' He quantified the vendor bloat: 'if I look at facilities management plumbing, probably have over 300 vendors providing plumbing service. Is that the right number? Can we rationalize that by regions, by states?' He stated the team capacity constraint: 'my addressable spend today should be around $600M. Am I able to touch all of them today? No. With the resource I have (half dozen people) I'm not able to, so I'd like to keep growing my team so we can address more of the addressable spend.' On contract management he described a completely manual, at-risk process: 'today I created my own repository on Smartsheet... I create some automations to give me a heads up when the contract is getting close to expiration, if there is any milestones, but this is all I can do. I'm looking for a CLM tool with repository, contract visibility, version controls, AI-powered clause libraries, manage approvals workflow and redlining, external party collaboration, integrated with the relevant stakeholder notes confirm 'missing entries in Smartsheet still lead to unintentional auto-renewals' and 'contract redlining performed in Microsoft Word and SharePoint with multiple email-based rounds and manual consolidation.' The pain is operational (manual, time-intensive, error-prone processes), financial (inability to enforce contract compliance and capture savings from vendor rationalization), and strategic (cannot scale procurement coverage to the $600M addressable spend with current tools and headcount). Pain owner is Leandro and his sourcing team; Jon Gappa (SVP) as the senior executive is accountable for the transformation. Pain is confirmed, specific, quantified (300+ plumbing vendors, $600M addressable spend, 1-2 days manual spend analysis), and tied to the buyer's strategic goal of moving from tactical purchasing to strategic sourcing.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a structured, multi-vendor RFP process with internal business-case approval as the gate to vendor selection. Leandro confirmed on 31 Mar 'I will probably go to a formal RFP' when asked by Karson about process. Duane stated on the same call that the team is 'evaluating a few other tools as well, just in the beginning trying to understand what's out there in the market, get a sense of the price so we can create the business case and press to the organization,' and that 'budget approvals are pending case justification.' The call sequence shows a phased process: early discovery (6 Mar), full 90-minute multi-stakeholder demo (31 Mar with 7 attendees including SVP Jon Gappa), ongoing commercial input and pricing discussions (Apr-May per Next Step), and now (as of 10 Jun) waiting on Leandro to return from PTO to continue commercial follow-up. Karson offered on 31 Mar to 'share a couple of templates for an RFP' and to 'help support building the business case to show reduction in cycle time, visibility, etc. to sell internally,' which Leandro accepted. The approval chain involves procurement (Leandro, Duane, Jon Gappa as SVP), legal (flagged by Karson on 31 Mar; Leandro said 'I'm keeping it more procurement focused for now, later we'll need to bring the legal team'), finance (implied by business case and budget approval requirement), and IT (data security and AI governance raised on demo call). The process is: complete vendor demos → issue formal RFP → receive proposals and pricing → down-select → build business case with winning vendor → secure budget approval from finance/exec → contracting with legal/IT/procurement sign-off. Cinemark is currently between demo completion and RFP issuance, with pricing input still being finalized. Timeline: Leandro stated ideal Q3 2026 implementation but acknowledged 'depending on so many factors it could be Q1 next year.' The process is clear, multi-gated, and vendor-neutral (RFP); Zycus has completed the demo gate successfully and is awaiting RFP issuance.

decision_criteria.narrative: Leandro articulated detailed, multi-module evaluation criteria across the two discovery calls and the 31 Mar demo. For Spend Analytics: multi-source data consolidation (Workday, MS Dynamics, regional satellite systems), AI-powered vendor normalization and parent-child rollup, custom procurement taxonomy separate from finance's chart of accounts, supplier rationalization insights by region and category, contract compliance visibility (spend against contracted suppliers), and dashboards surfacing savings opportunities. For CLM: central contract repository replacing manual Smartsheet, AI-powered clause libraries and risk analysis, version control, redlining workflow with the relevant stakeholder integration, external party collaboration, Workday integration for contract-vs-spend enforcement, and automated renewal alerts to prevent unintended auto-renewals. For tail-spend: autonomous negotiation agent (Anna) to handle low-value RFQs with minimal procurement intervention, evaluated on ROI feasibility. On the 31 Mar demo Duane confirmed 'consolidated spend analytics, contract lifecycle management, and AI-driven negotiation align with Cinemark's key pain points.' Additional criteria surfaced on demo: configurable approval workflows by contract type and value bands to match internal governance (high-value agreements escalate to senior reviewers), and data security/AI governance (IT flagged that all AI models must run in a private tenant with no Cinemark data leaving that environment). Leandro also confirmed 31 Mar the scope is 'mainly spend analytics, contract management, and potentially moving through e-sourcing,' explicitly excluding full source-to-pay to avoid replacing Workday. The criteria are detailed, consistent across calls, and tied to confirmed pain. Zycus demonstrated every named capability on 31 Mar and received positive acknowledgment ('definitely covers the vast majority of our pain points' per call notes). The evaluation is criterion-driven and Zycus has shown fit; the remaining decision work is competitive benchmarking (RFP) and business case approval.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Cinemark is AI Curious with strong signals of AI Hungry behavior. Leandro explicitly named AI as a requirement across spend analytics ('AI powered insights, vendor normalization'), CLM ('AI-powered clause libraries'), and tail-spend management ('we are really open for agent AI, we still need to understand the ROI'). He is not passively accepting AI as table stakes; he is evaluating it as a decision criterion and asking ROI questions, which signals genuine interest in adoption if the value case is proven. IT raised AI governance as a gate (private tenant, data residency), which confirms organizational readiness to deploy AI but with controls. The buyer did not resist or downplay AI on any call; they leaned in. AIS fields are null in Salesforce (no AIS_Score__c or AIS_Status__c), so no contradictory system signal. Calls show Cinemark is AI Curious leaning Hungry; the move is to push them fully into Hungry by quantifying AI ROI (vendor rationalization savings, tail-spend capture, contract risk reduction) in the business case.

competitive_position.summary: Cinemark is running a multi-vendor, RFP-gated evaluation of enterprise spend analytics and CLM platforms; no specific competitor names have been disclosed by the buyer. Leandro confirmed 31 Mar 'we are evaluating a few other tools' and 'I will probably go to a formal RFP,' establishing that Zycus is one of several vendors in a benchmarking phase. The likely field (inferred from scope and market) includes Coupa, Ivalua, SAP Ariba, Jaggaer, and GEP, but this is inference only. Zycus has completed discovery and a well-received 90-minute demo (31 Mar, 7 stakeholders, positive feedback) and is now in commercial follow-up; other vendors are presumably at similar or earlier stages. The single strongest CURRENT threat is the RFP process itself, which resets competitive position to a feature/price matrix and delays decision until all vendors respond and Cinemark completes internal business case approval. No vendor is identified as the incumbent or front-runner. Zycus's position: demonstrated fit on every stated criterion (spend consolidation, AI vendor normalization, CLM with Word integration, autonomous negotiation, Workday integration), strong champion (Leandro), multi-threaded (7 stakeholders on demo), and engaged as of 18 Jun. The wedge to win: co-author the business case with quantified ROI (vendor rationalization savings, cycle time reduction, contract compliance lift) so Zycus becomes the vendor that helps Cinemark secure budget approval, not just a compliant RFP respondent. That business case co-creation is the highest-leverage move before RFP issuance.

hard.ais_why:
• The March 31 2026 demo confirmed alignment on the AI value proposition: consolidated spend analytics, AI-driven CLM and autonomous negotiation were validated against Cinemark's pain points. The buyer acknowledged the AI fit but IT raised a governance constraint requiring AI models to run in a private tenant with no Cinemark data leaving that environment. That is sophisticated AI engagement with guardrails.
• Buyer questions on AutoClass AI classification, Merlin intake orchestration via Teams, MS Word InstaReview for clause-level risk, and Anna autonomous negotiation for tail-spend were active but reactive to the vendor flow. SVP Jon Gappa was in the room with Senior Directors Damasio and Flowers, with the close framed as comparing Zycus against other solutions rather than committing to AI as the differentiator.
• AI importance is rated Neutral and the description records no AI adoption currently with prospect curious in an ROI-driven posture. Four meetings on record, including a deep-dive 89-minute session, but the messaging is about building a generic procurement business case rather than an AI-led transformation. With no AI champion, this sits cleanly on the Curious side.

Differentiate on single-platform architecture (vs. stitched acquisitions) enabling true spend-to-contract enforcement, AI agents (autonomous negotiation Anna, InstaReview clause risk) as category-leading capability, and Microsoft Word + Teams + Workday integration exactly matching their stated environment. Convert Leandro's operational advocacy into Jon Gappa (SVP) executive sponsorship by demonstrating strategic ROI (scale procurement coverage toward the $600M addressable spend without proportional headcount growth). Make the RFP response a formality because the business case already names Zycus as the recommended solution.

value.flag: Co-author the quantified business case NOW with Leandro and Duane before RFP issuance. Cinemark's budget approval is gated by a business case they are still building ('we need to create the business case and press to the organization,' Duane 31 Mar). The vendor who delivers that business case with them (not just offers to help) becomes the recommended solution and makes the RFP a formality. Model the ROI: X% reduction in manual spend-analysis effort (Leandro: '1-2 days to get contract compliance rate'), Y% vendor rationalization savings (300+ plumbing vendors → consolidated regional contracts), Z% tail-spend capture via autonomous agent (3-7% on tail per demo), contract compliance lift (unknown rate today → 95%+ visibility), and cycle time reduction (Smartsheet manual alerts → automated renewals, email-based redlining → in-Word real-time collaboration). Use Cinemark's $600M addressable spend and half-dozen person team as the baseline. Deliver a one-page executive summary with 3-year NPV and a 10-slide board deck Leandro and Duane can present to Jon Gappa and finance. This is the single highest-impact move: it shifts Zycus from 'RFP respondent' to 'strategic partner who secured our budget.'

value.flag: Convert Leandro's operational advocacy into Jon Gappa (SVP Sourcing) executive sponsorship by framing the deal as the enabler of Cinemark's procurement transformation. Jon Gappa attended the 31 Mar demo but has not been the primary contact; all commercial and follow-up discussions have been with the relevant stakeholder is the most likely budget authority or one gate below it, and he is the stakeholder who will champion the business case internally to finance and exec leadership. Schedule a 30-minute executive alignment call (Karson + VP Alexa Bradley, or escalate to the deal owner's manager if the deal size and stage warrant it) with Jon Gappa and Leandro in the next 10 days to frame the strategic narrative: Zycus as the platform that scales procurement coverage toward the $600M addressable spend without proportional headcount growth, enabling the shift from tactical purchasing to strategic sourcing Leandro named as Cinemark's goal. Use the call to confirm Jon's timeline expectations (Q3 2026 realistic or Q1 2027 more likely?), surface any unstated concerns (budget constraints, competing priorities, IT integration complexity), and secure his explicit support for the business case and RFP recommendation. An EB who has been framed as the transformation champion will drive the RFP outcome, not just score it.

value.flag: Proactively address the IT and legal gates before they become late-cycle blockers. IT flagged data security and AI governance on the demo ('all AI models must run in a private tenant, no Cinemark data leaves that environment'), and legal will join 'later' per Leandro but has not been engaged yet. Both are approval gates that can stall or kill a deal in contracting if concerns surface late. In the next 14 days: (1) send IT a one-page AI governance and data residency FAQ answering the private tenant question, plus a security white paper and SOC 2 attestation, and offer a 30-min technical deep-dive if they want it; (2) send Leandro a sample MSA, Order Form, and SOW (redacted from a comparable customer) so legal can review structure and standard terms in parallel with the RFP process, not sequentially after. Proactive transparency de-risks the back-end and signals Zycus is easy to contract with, a competitive differentiator when the buyer is evaluating multiple vendors on equal functional fit.
